contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: MICHIGAN From the Signal of Liberty LETTER FROM JAMES G BIRNEY Detroit Sept 291841 Dear Sir Some three weeks since private business called me to Saginaw in this Stale I did not intend delivering any AntiSlavery addresses on my route How ever on arriving at this place I was soon called upon by some of our friends who thought that even a single lecture might be of some service to the cause here This was before dinner and as my traveling companions required me to continue tny journey next morning 1 consented to de liver a lecture that evening Notwith standing the notice was so short we had quite a large assembly at the Presbyterian church The next Sunday I spent at Flint in Genesee county 1 had no personal ac quaintance in tire place I found out how ever that tbtre were abolitionists in the village and not a few in the vicinity Next day before leaving for Saginaw 1 was called on by some of them and requested to lecture that evening As 1 could not remain with any convenience all night I promised to lecture an evening on my re turn I returned on the 21th mst
